Every donation adds up and helps support John’s work in the U.S. Senate so we can finally make real progress on the issues that impact us the most. Thank you for your support, Derek. — Team Fetterman 💛 | | Email us: info@johnfetterman.com

name: Jayapal HQ
subject: You are not an ATM
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Pramila for Congress
body:
#
Peter,
Our team has set a critical February fundraising goal that we need to reach by the end of the month in order to stay strong. **Here’s why we’re asking for two minutes of your time and a donation.**
Messages just like this are one of the main ways we communicate with Pramila’s most active supporters -- that's you, Peter! It’s people like you that power our grassroots campaign year-round and help grow our movement.
We are _not_ here to scare you into making a contribution -- that’s not cool. We won’t pretend that the whole world will end if we don’t meet this specific fundraising goal. **But we do want you to know that emails like this have a real impact on our fundraising,** and your donation at this time is much appreciated.
**We’ve done the math and set a $70,000 goal for February.** That’s a lot of money! [**But with the help of grassroots supporters like you, we can close the gap. Will you donate $3 (or whatever you can) now toward our February goal?**](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/38408?refcode=em20230225-2209&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=2209%2E420207%2ELi5Alh)
**[Chip in »](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/38408?refcode=em20230225-2209&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=20&akid=2209%2E420207%2ELi5Alh)**
**We don’t believe that our supporters are ATMs and that’s why we keep it real with you.** Our fundraising could be in a stronger place, which is exactly why we set an ambitious goal to keep us on track and in a strong position for the cycle.
Our goal is straightforward: raise enough to get back on track with the plan we’ve set for 2023. **That means we need $70,000 by 11:59pm Tuesday.**
Every donation, no matter how big or small, adds up for us, and we take that seriously. Without you, we wouldn’t be able to maintain Pramila’s campaign as she fights for us all from the halls of Congress.
[**We’re asking you to stand with Pramila at this critical point and pitch in to help us get back on track and reach $70,000. Can you donate $3 or more today?**](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/38408?refcode=em20230225-2209&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=21&akid=2209%2E420207%2ELi5Alh)
Thank you for always supporting Pramila,
-- Jayapal HQ

**[Contribute](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pj-email-footer?refcode=em20230225-2209-footer&t=22&refcode2=2209_420207_Li5Alh&akid=2209%2E420207%2ELi5Alh)**
---
[](https://www.facebook.com/pramila.jayapal) | [](https://twitter.com/pramilajayapal) | [](https://www.instagram.com/pramilajayapal/)
Email us: info@pramilaforcongress.com
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, and small-dollar contributions sustain our movement for transformative change. To contribute by check, please address to Pramila for Congress, PO Box 21912, Seattle, WA 98111.
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/)
* [Update My Personal Information](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/signup/did-your-info-change/?t=23&akid=2209%2E420207%2ELi5Alh)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=24&akid=2209%2E420207%2ELi5Alh)

Paid for by Pramila for Congress

name: Lizzie Pannill Fletcher
subject: First, they came for Roe. Now, they’re coming after our right to travel.
body:
ELIZABETH PANNILL FLETCHER FOR CONGRESS [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP10/1/82676/images/lizzie-reelect-logo-outlined.png]
This month, I reintroduced my bill to protect Americans’ constitutional right to travel freely between states for reproductive health care.
The Supreme Court’s decision to overturn Roe v. Wade last year was not the end its proponents had long claimed it was. It is the beginning of a new phase of the all-out, nationwide assault on reproductive freedom. Now, 16 states — including Texas — have banned or severely limited access to abortion, which has forced people who can to travel to get the reproductive health care they need.

https://secure.actblue.com/donate/folf230224?refcode=folf230224 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/folf230224?refcode=folf230224]

In response, anti-choice politicians and leaders coming for our constitutional right to travel between the states, too.
That’s right — lawmakers here in Texas and around the country want to restrict people’s ability to travel for the reproductive care they need and are introducing legislation to make that the law.
The future that these lawmakers envision is really unimaginable to most of us in Texas – it is at odds with our fundamental values as Texans and Americans. It is an assault on our freedom, privacy, dignity, and health. But they are working every day to make this vision a reality.
That’s why it’s so important for us to fight back. I promised to do everything I can to protect reproductive freedom — including the freedom to travel for the care we need — and that’s why I made one of my first actions in this new Congress reintroducing my bill protecting the right to travel for abortion. We also need to fight back at the ballot box.
We’ll need a sustained, systemic, strategic effort to take back our state from anti-choice extremists who don’t represent the majority of Texans. That’s why we must continue our work together, and I’m so grateful to know I can count on you to do this with us.
Together, we can do anything,
Lizzie Sign-off [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP10/1/82676/images/lizzie_signature-1.png]

DONATE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/folf230224?refcode=folf230224]

name: Team Katie Porter
subject: Where Republican priorities lie
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Katie Porter for Senate
body:
In their first month in power, House Republicans passed a bill that would rescind the IRS’ funding
# [](https://act.katieporter.com/go/14558?t=1&akid=18355%2E3422442%2E5nNFYq)
In the nearly two months of a GOP-led House, we’ve seen where Republican priorities lie: with their ultra-wealthy donors and multinational corporations.
**[Sign our petition today to show your support for a fully-funded IRS, so that major corporations and the wealthiest few pay their fair share in taxes.](https://act.katieporter.com/signup/kp4s_em_irs/?source=em20230225-1&akid=18355%2E3422442%2E5nNFYq)**
**[Sign now ›››](https://act.katieporter.com/signup/kp4s_em_irs/?source=EMAF25_1_IRS_20230225_V1_F&akid=18355%2E3422442%2E5nNFYq)**
**A quick recap:** Last year, Congress passed a bill into law that boosted IRS funding, allowing the IRS to go after wealthy tax-cheats and provide better customer service to everyday taxpayers—helping you get a faster refund or being able to answer questions.
**Why this was necessary:** The IRS was defunded by Republicans in 2010, allowing ultra-wealthy people to go without audits and empowering corporations to cheat. With 50,000 IRS workers set to retire soon, we needed to get new personnel trained and ready to simply keep the IRS running.
**But in their first month in power, House Republicans passed a bill that would**** _rescind_****this much-needed IRS funding.** The only ones who benefit from an under-funded IRS are big corporations who evade taxes. When corporations avoid taxes, working people are left to pick up the slack, and the Republican bill would have added $114 billion (!) to the deficit over ten years.
Katie was proud to oppose this bill. It won’t become law any time soon thanks to our Senate majority and President Biden—**but this is a clear example of who Republicans fight for when they have power.**
**[Show your support for a fully funded IRS by signing our petition. Let’s show Republicans that people won’t be fooled by their fear-mongering.](https://act.katieporter.com/signup/kp4s_em_irs/?source=em20230225-1&akid=18355%2E3422442%2E5nNFYq)**
**[Sign now ›››](https://act.katieporter.com/signup/kp4s_em_irs/?source=EMAF25_1_IRS_20230225_V1_F&akid=18355%2E3422442%2E5nNFYq)**
Onward,
Team Katie Porter

Katie is running for the U.S. Senate because Californians deserve a warrior fighting for them in Washington. Our campaign is people-powered, we don’t take a dime from corporate PACs, federal lobbyists, or executives from Big Oil or Big Pharma. **[Donate ›››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4s-em-footer?refcode=EMAF25_1_IRS_20230225_V1_F&t=2&refcode2=18355_3422442_5nNFYq&akid=18355%2E3422442%2E5nNFYq)**
---
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.katieporter.com/signup/receive-fewer-kp4s-emails/?t=3&akid=18355%2E3422442%2E5nNFYq)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.katieporter.com/signup/kp4s-em-update-info-footer)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.katieporter.com/unsubscribe/kp4s-unsubscribe/?t=4&akid=18355%2E3422442%2E5nNFYq)

To give by check:
Katie Porter for Senate
PO Box 5176
Irvine, CA 92616
Email us: [info@katieporter.com](mailto:info@katieporter.com)
Paid for by Katie Porter for Senate

| Senator Amanda Chase
---
Senator Chase has represented Chesterfield, Colonial Heights and Amelia Counties in the Senate since first elected in 2015, almost 8 years. She grew up in Bon Air and has lived most of her life in Chesterfield, since 1979.
She has been rated as one of the most conservative Senators in Virginia by the American Conservative Union and has a lifetime score of 100% with the Family Foundation, VCDL and A with the NRA.
She is also a champion in the General Assembly for pushing back against vaccine and mask mandates, calling for a full investigation into the 2020 Presidential Election, parental rights in education, medical freedom, protecting gun rights and help for our veterans.

| 2023 SESSION WEEK 7 OVERVIEW

Dear Friends, The Virginia Senate officially adjourned today, Saturday, February 25 at 5pm. We have completed our work for the 2023 legislative session on all of our bills.
Unfortunately, we have not fully completed our work on the budget and we will have to return for a Special Session to be called by the Governor. Today, we passed a "skinny budget," which is one of the few budgets I've supported over the course of 8 years. In my opinion, we need more "skinny budgets" with less spending and more tax cutting.
As for now, all legislators are headed back home to run for re-election, until reconvene for the purposes of unfinished business on Wednesday, April 12. Now that we have adjourned, we can continue to raise money to run for re-election. Please consider making a [donation today](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001ftduTdVWaHGM7afx2XSkbkiPao1j60BTCGvNmnQBTloCof6RT3EWOxFgloqQgDsZBmKoVe0cTc4eekf6A4P-DEm50qyro9R32FnndWV9wfffIHgyWq09Z_YMZgVePNZi8G5UDlmZtJCvXWMb7WGJZZzS2JZz7PFzMWCr_k38YqklD94ngHv8S-gj4EesLdZbNwyMbJioT8yVycPs0xk2ng==&c=BtSmSOgf-X2J4USbI_NVOtnfXKKEAH3ttIKv8i7X0czoYvkeSOC90g==&ch=3sRpLUl3cIgkwztcCEu97-3KLNu-RO00Oq5TEqRHnk1zctJTymHYdA==)[.](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001ftduTdVWaHGM7afx2XSkbkiPao1j60BTCGvNmnQBTloCof6RT3EWOwWRB-rm6qAVmpCF4vuot0MnhOoIn4fX5d5YPud0w96wWTDl4SsKM5CvNCigDB2o3sGCEZMLnt9c4lloMuzR59mwaTJ0Fne-AgPd3n5YFmecQ8583AzN39Q=&c=BtSmSOgf-X2J4USbI_NVOtnfXKKEAH3ttIKv8i7X0czoYvkeSOC90g==&ch=3sRpLUl3cIgkwztcCEu97-3KLNu-RO00Oq5TEqRHnk1zctJTymHYdA==)
Between now and April 12 the Governor will review bills that have passed the General Assembly and chose to sign, veto or amend these bills. When the General Assembly returns on April 12 for "reconvene," also called the "veto" session, we will approve or reject the Governor's amendments or attempt to override any vetoes.
But first, here's a quick assessment of bills that actually survived. There are not many. Almost all of the most controversial bills put forward died, due to the split assembly. Here are a few bills that did pass both the House and Senate, several of these bills I was a copatron as noted:

1. A bill passed that would allow gun owners to claim a tax rebate of up to $300 dollars for buying a firearm lockbox or somewhere to store their firearm safely. And that would go into effect in this tax year and would last five years. And again, you could claim up to $300 dollars.
2. A bill passed that was carried by Sen. [Steve Newman](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001ftduTdVWaHGM7afx2XSkbkiPao1j60BTCGvNmnQBTloCof6RT3EWOxFgloqQgDsZcdCvDnty0aaa7qJM51z9_VpVQjeCUa_HwSAG8UhJY8ZysjzaVSlRk600uEvsRQ9LsLDOOgOGS_N6EAm0l4s_bDz0HetLc5czKP4mC2LMFF0AhKe8qhJRp_c3RHGOiX0W__p2kC75sO_kuvY-I5XQZRUKsSCngIVW&c=BtSmSOgf-X2J4USbI_NVOtnfXKKEAH3ttIKv8i7X0czoYvkeSOC90g==&ch=3sRpLUl3cIgkwztcCEu97-3KLNu-RO00Oq5TEqRHnk1zctJTymHYdA==) , R-Bedford, and I served as a co-sponsor, that would allow colleges and universities to access health and criminal history records of students who pose a significant threat on campus. Virginia's colleges and universities would be permitted to obtain criminal histories and health records of people seen as posing a "significant" threat on campuses . This legislation was filed in response to the November shooting at the University of Virginia that left three football players dead.
3. A bill passed from Sen. Richard Stuart, R-King George, to prohibit foreign governments from making agricultural land purchases. This is probably my favorite bill that passed this year. Under the bill, the ban would apply to foreign governments or others the U.S. Secretary of Commerce has determined "to have engaged in a long-term pattern or serious instances of conduct significantly adverse to the national security of the United States or security and safety of United States persons." The bill would impose the ban starting Jan. 1, 2023, meaning it would go into effect once it is signed, but it would not void farmland purchases made before 2023. The legislation also requires the Virginia Department of Agriculture and Consumer Services to provide a yearly report to the governor and General Assembly on the farmland owned by foreign governments. The [current list of "foreign adversaries"](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001ftduTdVWaHGM7afx2XSkbkiPao1j60BTCGvNmnQBTloCof6RT3EWOxFgloqQgDsZ7ut-oAW25qmbfRh5GETvjZ6NUgQYlKjEpn8pjGQKIiYHCWl5x7b3iHTqDtP6-sy5y7gz2iABZ8VJ8FcplnyeAogkoKEunWXO6tLSDUm9UiyizR7OLi3GH3q9OGJV5pgwxGOr3rVtkJs9th7oZB_y2mkrnFqtc0U_n3FSQPba8tM=&c=BtSmSOgf-X2J4USbI_NVOtnfXKKEAH3ttIKv8i7X0czoYvkeSOC90g==&ch=3sRpLUl3cIgkwztcCEu97-3KLNu-RO00Oq5TEqRHnk1zctJTymHYdA==) recognized by the U.S. includes The People's Republic of China, the Republic of Cuba, the Islamic Republic of Iran, North Korea, Russia and Venezuelan politician Nicolas Maduro. In 2021, the U.S. Department of Agriculture found foreign entities and persons held an interest in [nearly 41 million acres](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001ftduTdVWaHGM7afx2XSkbkiPao1j60BTCGvNmnQBTloCof6RT3EWOxFgloqQgDsZAdjRwvB0BCU-e0zlmc-B_s7Q7YGxTEIQzphyxiK6TubVSXEr8oRAjUr5bi8MQ2od73Qr2_DiqdD-cMpAWadUrHjKJuv-buHA9Gd8Z5ZaP99qiAWQ_SHcy__M4ZICmHdx&c=BtSmSOgf-X2J4USbI_NVOtnfXKKEAH3ttIKv8i7X0czoYvkeSOC90g==&ch=3sRpLUl3cIgkwztcCEu97-3KLNu-RO00Oq5TEqRHnk1zctJTymHYdA==) of the country's agricultural land , 3.1% of total privately owned land.
4. My seatmate Senator Travis Hackworth sponsored and I cosponsored a bill that passed, that prohibits any unmanned aircraft system from (i) dropping any item within the boundaries of or (ii) obtaining any videographic or still image of any identifiable inmate or resident at any state or local correctional facility or juvenile correctional center without consent or authorization. A violation of this prohibition is a Class 1 misdemeanor.
5. Senator Newman sponsored and I cosponsored a transportation bill that passed and allows funds to be used for any transportation project or any transportation facility within Virginia. These funds will be awarded by the Governor in the form of grants, revolving loans, or other financing tools and equity contributions. The Governor is authorized to award grants of up to $5 million dollars. Loans are interest-free and available up to the maximum of $30 million, based on funding availability. Loan terms vary but will not exceed seven years.
6. A bill passed that I cosponsored which directs the Department of Conservation and Recreation to establish a Virginia National Guard Passport that authorizes a member of the Virginia National Guard to enter state parks without paying a parking or admission fee.
7. My Republican seatmate Travis Hackworth sponsored a bill that I cosponsored and passed that would promote opportunities for energy development in Southwest Virginia, to create jobs and economic activity consistent with the Virginia Energy Plan, and to position Southwest Virginia and the Commonwealth as a leader in energy workforce and energy technology research and development.
8. Senator Tommy Norment passed a bill that requires review and approval of plans for changes to artifacts contained within the Capitol Building. This bill requires the Joint Committee on Rules to review all plans or proposals for alterations, improvements, additions, or renovations to, or other disposition of, any monuments, statuary, artwork, or other historical artifacts contained within the Capitol Building, including within the old Senate chamber, the old hall of the House of Delegates, and the Rotunda. The bill provides that no such plan or proposal shall be implemented without approval of a majority of Senate members and a majority of House members of the Joint Committee on Rules. The bill repeals the provisions of the Code of Virginia establishing the Capitol Square Preservation Council.

While there were many good bills that died. Thankfully, with a Republican controlled House, there were many bad bills that died. All attempts to infringe on your 2nd amendment rights were defeated.
Unfortunately, we were not able to repeal a law that links Virginia vehicle emissions to that of California. We absolutely must win back the House and Senate this year so that we can repeal this horrific mandate.
Now that the legislative session is complete other than the budget and veto session, election season begins in earnest. All 140 General Assembly seats will be up for grabs in November.
Let me emphasize that we're ALL up for re-election. That's 140 seats. 100 in the House and 40 in the Senate. All seats are in newly drawn districts in which many incumbent senators will actually face off against fellow incumbents or face retirement.
When we return in 2024, the General Assembly will have received a much needed overhaul and facelift. I call it "the Great General Assembly Reset". Senators Dick Saslaw, Janet Howell and Jill Vogel are just 3 of the 40 senators who have all publicly announced retirement. Because of the newly redrawn districts, most senators will face a challenger and more retirements are expected to be announced in the coming days.
I welcome the change in Richmond. I'm looking to a fresh new start with new faces and an opportunity to win back the Virginia Senate.
Thankfully, I'm the only incumbent running in Senate District 12 which consists of Chesterfield and Colonial Heights. I've represented most of this area for almost 8 years. That said, my former Senate seatmate Glenn Sturtevant is running against me. He's a nice guy with a bad voting record. Tina Ramirez decided to run for state senate after several unsuccessful runs for Congress. That said, we are not taking anything for granted and are now running full speed ahead towards the Tuesday, June 20 primary. Please see the list below for upcoming events and volunteer opportunities. Here are some highlights from this past week.

She is courageously ahead of the curve in introducing and shepherding into law issues that meet peoples needs rather than seeking out easy and non-controversial issues.
We need Amanda for another term in the Virginia Senate."
---
| Former Chesterfield Commonwealth Attorney Billy Davenport
---
| |
---
"I have had the pleasure of knowing Senator Amanda Chase for the past fifteen years.
She is a Christian lady who lives her life by "Founding Father Values", the values that made our Country great."
---
| "She stands firm on our Constitutional Rights and amid much adversity she never wavers on the First and Second Amendments which give us the free exercise of religion, freedom of speech, the press, and the right to peacefully assemble to and petition the government for a redress of grievances, and the right to keep and bear arms.
Without the first two amendments all the other amendments would have much less effect.
She will not be silenced as she forcefully and effectively represents the people of her district.
She is a true Statesman who will not violate her oath of office and the trust of her constituents."
---
| Former Chesterfield Commissioner of Revenue Joe Horbal
---
| |
---
"I endorse Virginia State Senator Amanda Chase as she seeks re-election to the Senate of Virginia in Senate District 12.
I've known Amanda both professionally and personally for over 15 years and know her to be a person of good character and integrity. "
---
| "Even before she was in the state senate she consistently stood up for what was right and wasn't afraid to challenge the status quo.
That said, she knows how to get things done and is a reliable and consistent vote for the people.
She stands strongly for the Constitution and doing right by the people she represents.
Amanda has been an effective Virginia State Senator who has consistently represented us well and without influence by special interest groups.
She has accomplished much since she was first elected in 2015. She works tirelessly for the people to represent their needs and we need to re-elect her to the Virginia Senate to be our voice."

---
| Dick Heller, D.C. vs. Heller, Supreme Court Gun Freedom Case

| In order to get my name on the ballot we must get at least 250 registered voters who live in [Senate District 12 ](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001ftduTdVWaHGM7afx2XSkbkiPao1j60BTCGvNmnQBTloCof6RT3EWOzIuOiy0BHlTn1O9ufLJXV4eWokBttxX-k-y9ax3_pzOGfQ8XPTFvgqC87rzy90V5y4PAtZqsY2VfuoAhA_iAWuLvRhWupRbY9SeJgSXPb0qPm154HGNQby0vawekLGdDe6S4qnkAuETWyKijpI8X4GOZX7XF2mUlSszNTS8I3rWfWPR-atrGYlqFNQhMC1GXP4fOS2hpyDe&c=BtSmSOgf-X2J4USbI_NVOtnfXKKEAH3ttIKv8i7X0czoYvkeSOC90g==&ch=3sRpLUl3cIgkwztcCEu97-3KLNu-RO00Oq5TEqRHnk1zctJTymHYdA==) to sign my petition. This is something I have to do every time I've run for re-election.
Thanks to all who have already signed and asked to collect signatures for us.
If you'd like to sign my petition, you can stop by my senate office, see me at church on Sunday in the foyer in between services or attend one of our upcoming events like our Tea Party meetings, held on the first of each month.
We can also email you the form. You can ask family members to sign the petition and mail the form back to us, but please remember to get the form notarized at your bank before you mail it back to us.
We must turn in our signatures before Monday, March 20 at noon sharp to the GOP District Chair. You will want to turn in your notarized forms well before this date, as this is the date we must turn all forms into the chair.
Please reach out to Mike at Mike@chaseforva.com if you have questions or call our campaign cell at 804 380 0009.

clean_body: From the Desk of Senator Chase | | | | | | == | CUT TO THE CHASE WITH SENATOR CHASE Weekly Session Newsletter Week 7 of 7 We have adjourned! | | | | | | WHAT HAPPENED THIS WEEK? | WEEK 7: GENERAL ASSEMBLY UPDATE | | | Senator Amanda Chase Senator Chase has represented Chesterfield, Colonial Heights and Amelia Counties in the Senate since first elected in 2015, almost 8 years. She grew up in Bon Air and has lived most of her life in Chesterfield, since 1979. She has been rated as one of the most conservative Senators in Virginia by the American Conservative Union and has a lifetime score of 100% with the Family Foundation, VCDL and A with the NRA. She is also a champion in the General Assembly for pushing back against vaccine and mask mandates, calling for a full investigation into the 2020 Presidential Election, parental rights in education, medical freedom, protecting gun rights and help for our veterans. | WE'VE OFFICIALLY ADJORNED THE FINAL WEEK | | | == | 2023 SESSION WEEK 7 OVERVIEW Dear Friends, The Virginia Senate officially adjourned today, Saturday, February 25 at 5pm. We have completed our work for the 2023 legislative session on all of our bills. Unfortunately, we have not fully completed our work on the budget and we will have to return for a Special Session to be called by the Governor. Today, we passed a "skinny budget," which is one of the few budgets I've supported over the course of 8 years. In my opinion, we need more "skinny budgets" with less spending and more tax cutting. As for now, all legislators are headed back home to run for re-election, until reconvene for the purposes of unfinished business on Wednesday, April 12. Now that we have adjourned, we can continue to raise money to run for re-election. Please consider making a == Between now and April 12 the Governor will review bills that have passed the General Assembly and chose to sign, veto or amend these bills. When the General Assembly returns on April 12 for "reconvene," also called the "veto" session, we will approve or reject the Governor's amendments or attempt to override any vetoes. But first, here's a quick assessment of bills that actually survived. There are not many. Almost all of the most controversial bills put forward died, due to the split assembly. Here are a few bills that did pass both the House and Senate, several of these bills I was a copatron as noted: 1. A bill passed that would allow gun owners to claim a tax rebate of up to $300 dollars for buying a firearm lockbox or somewhere to store their firearm safely. And that would go into effect in this tax year and would last five years. And again, you could claim up to $300 dollars. 2. A bill passed that was carried by Sen. == , R-Bedford, and I served as a co-sponsor, that would allow colleges and universities to access health and criminal history records of students who pose a significant threat on campus. Virginia's colleges and universities would be permitted to obtain criminal histories and health records of people seen as posing a "significant" threat on campuses . This legislation was filed in response to the November shooting at the University of Virginia that left three football players dead. 3. A bill passed from Sen. Richard Stuart, R-King George, to prohibit foreign governments from making agricultural land purchases. This is probably my favorite bill that passed this year. Under the bill, the ban would apply to foreign governments or others the U.S. Secretary of Commerce has determined "to have engaged in a long-term pattern or serious instances of conduct significantly adverse to the national security of the United States or security and safety of United States persons." The bill would impose the ban starting Jan. 1, 2023, meaning it would go into effect once it is signed, but it would not void farmland purchases made before 2023. The legislation also requires the Virginia Department of Agriculture and Consumer Services to provide a yearly report to the governor and General Assembly on the farmland owned by foreign governments. The == recognized by the U.S. includes The People's Republic of China, the Republic of Cuba, the Islamic Republic of Iran, North Korea, Russia and Venezuelan politician Nicolas Maduro. In 2021, the U.S. Department of Agriculture found foreign entities and persons held an interest in == of the country's agricultural land , 3.1% of total privately owned land. 4. My seatmate Senator Travis Hackworth sponsored and I cosponsored a bill that passed, that prohibits any unmanned aircraft system from (i) dropping any item within the boundaries of or (ii) obtaining any videographic or still image of any identifiable inmate or resident at any state or local correctional facility or juvenile correctional center without consent or authorization. A violation of this prohibition is a Class 1 misdemeanor. 5. Senator Newman sponsored and I cosponsored a transportation bill that passed and allows funds to be used for any transportation project or any transportation facility within Virginia. These funds will be awarded by the Governor in the form of grants, revolving loans, or other financing tools and equity contributions. The Governor is authorized to award grants of up to $5 million dollars. Loans are interest-free and available up to the maximum of $30 million, based on funding availability. Loan terms vary but will not exceed seven years. 6. A bill passed that I cosponsored which directs the Department of Conservation and Recreation to establish a Virginia National Guard Passport that authorizes a member of the Virginia National Guard to enter state parks without paying a parking or admission fee. 7. My Republican seatmate Travis Hackworth sponsored a bill that I cosponsored and passed that would promote opportunities for energy development in Southwest Virginia, to create jobs and economic activity consistent with the Virginia Energy Plan, and to position Southwest Virginia and the Commonwealth as a leader in energy workforce and energy technology research and development. 8. Senator Tommy Norment passed a bill that requires review and approval of plans for changes to artifacts contained within the Capitol Building. This bill requires the Joint Committee on Rules to review all plans or proposals for alterations, improvements, additions, or renovations to, or other disposition of, any monuments, statuary, artwork, or other historical artifacts contained within the Capitol Building, including within the old Senate chamber, the old hall of the House of Delegates, and the Rotunda. The bill provides that no such plan or proposal shall be implemented without approval of a majority of Senate members and a majority of House members of the Joint Committee on Rules. The bill repeals the provisions of the Code of Virginia establishing the Capitol Square Preservation Council. While there were many good bills that died. Thankfully, with a Republican controlled House, there were many bad bills that died. All attempts to infringe on your 2nd amendment rights were defeated. Unfortunately, we were not able to repeal a law that links Virginia vehicle emissions to that of California. We absolutely must win back the House and Senate this year so that we can repeal this horrific mandate. Now that the legislative session is complete other than the budget and veto session, election season begins in earnest. All 140 General Assembly seats will be up for grabs in November. Let me emphasize that we're ALL up for re-election. That's 140 seats. 100 in the House and 40 in the Senate. All seats are in newly drawn districts in which many incumbent senators will actually face off against fellow incumbents or face retirement. When we return in 2024, the General Assembly will have received a much needed overhaul and facelift. I call it "the Great General Assembly Reset". Senators Dick Saslaw, Janet Howell and Jill Vogel are just 3 of the 40 senators who have all publicly announced retirement. Because of the newly redrawn districts, most senators will face a challenger and more retirements are expected to be announced in the coming days. I welcome the change in Richmond. I'm looking to a fresh new start with new faces and an opportunity to win back the Virginia Senate. Thankfully, I'm the only incumbent running in Senate District 12 which consists of Chesterfield and Colonial Heights. I've represented most of this area for almost 8 years. That said, my former Senate seatmate Glenn Sturtevant is running against me. He's a nice guy with a bad voting record. Tina Ramirez decided to run for state senate after several unsuccessful runs for Congress. That said, we are not taking anything for granted and are now running full speed ahead towards the Tuesday, June 20 primary. Please see the list below for upcoming events and volunteer opportunities. Here are some highlights from this past week. | Bus Tour with John Frederick and Delegate John McGuire for State Senate in Amelia | | | | | | | | | | | | | Rally for Bishop Leon Benjamin with Kim Klacik and Joe Collins in Chesterfield | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Fundraiser for Leon Benjamin with General Mike Flynn | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | I humbly ask for your support as I run for re-election to the Virginia Senate | WHERE IS THE 12th SENATE DISTRICT? | The 12th Senatorial District includes most of Chesterfield and all of Colonial Heights. Click on the link below to see a map for each precinct within the district. | | | == | | | | == | | | ---|--- | Please Sign The Petition to Get My Name on the Ballot | In order to get my name on the ballot we must get at least 250 registered voters who live in == to sign my petition. This is something I have to do every time I've run for re-election. Senate District 12 includes most of Chesterfield and all of Colonial Heights. Do you live in Bon Air, North Chesterfield, Woodlake, Summerlake or Smoketree? You live in SD12. For a complete map of the district please click the link above. You must actually sign a physical petition. This is not something that can be done online. You can sign in person at our next event or we can email the form to you. If we mail you the form, you can ask family members to sign the petition and mail the form back to us, but please remember to get the form notarized at your bank before you mail it back to us. Here's our address: Friends of Amanda Chase Attention: Petition PO Box 5811 Midlothian, VA 23112 If you'd like to sign my petition, you can stop by my senate office, see me at church on Sunday in the foyer in between services or attend one of our upcoming events like our Tea Party meetings, held on the first of each month. Thanks to all who have already signed and asked to collect signatures for us. We must turn in our signatures before Monday, March 20 at noon sharp to the GOP District Chair. Please reach out to Mike at Mike@chaseforva.com. | DO YOU HAVE A GOOD SIGN LOCATION? | | Mike and I are putting up 4 by 8s across Senate District 12 which includes Chesterfield and Colonial Heights and will continue until all of our 4 by 8s have been placed. Please reach out to Mike if you have a good 4 by 8 location on a main road or want to help put up 4 by 8s at mike@chaseforva.com. Please remember to include the contact information of the property owner. We respect property rights and only put up signs where we have permission from the home or business owner. Thanks to all who've already provided such great locations on main thoroughfares throughout Chesterfield and Colonial Heights. The primary is Tuesday, June 20. If you'd like to add your business as a pick up location, please reach out to Mike at mike@chaseforva.com | | | ---|--- | Want a Yard Sign for your Yard? | Yard signs are also available for pick up at these location during their business hours: Bob Moate's Sports Shop 10418 Hull Street Road Midlothian, VA 23112 Hours: Monday-Thursday 8:30-6pm Friday 9-7pm Saturday 8-5pm Sunday 8-4pm Dance's Sporting Goods 570 Southpark Blvd Colonial Heights, VA 23834 Hours: Weekdays 8am to 6pm Saturday 10-4pm Sunday 12-4pm Or click here to request a sign: mailto:mike@chaseforva.com | ENDORSEMENTS | President Donald J Trump Congressman Bob Good Former Congressman Dave Brat Former Congressman Virgil Goode Bishop EW Jackson Delegate John McGuire Colonial Heights City Council John Wood Colonial Heights City Council Dr. Laura Poe Amelia County Sheriff Ricky Walker Arizona Senator Sonny Borrelli Pennsylvania Senator and Republican Nominee for Governor Doug Mastriano Former Chesterfield Board of Supervisors Don Sowder Former Chesterfield Commonwealth's Attorney Billy Davenport Former Chesterfield Commissioner of Revenue Joe Horbal John Frederick, The John Frederick Radio Network Dick Heller, D.C. vs. Heller, Supreme Court Gun Freedom Case | President Donald J Trump | == | "You're doing a great job... I'm going to be with YOU on your next campaign. Just let me know when you're ready." \- President Donald J Trump | Congressman Bob Good | | "Today, I'm announcing my official endorsement of Virginia State Senator Amanda Chase as she seeks re-election to the Virginia State Senate. | "Amanda is an unapologetic conservative who isn't afraid to take on the tough issues that are most important to the people. She's a solid vote that conservatives can count on and a trusted ally of the people she represents. She's a champion and a leader on so many issues here in Virginia and nationally to include election integrity, protecting our constitutional rights, like the Second Amendment, pushing back against mask and vaccine mandates and voting against all tax increases. We need to send our known fighter and firebrand back to the Virginia Senate so she can continue to make a difference in the lives of every Virginian." | Former Congressman Dave Brat | | "Today, I'm announcing my endorsement of Virginia State Senator Amanda Chase as she seeks re-election to the Virginia State Senate. Senator Chase supported me before it was cool to be Pro Faith, Pro Constitution, Pro Free Markets and Pro American. She has courage. I ran on and supported the Republican Creed of Virginia. | "Many say they support this platform, but not everyone votes like it or messages constantly on it like Senator Chase. • Free enterprise system - No special interests • All individuals are entitled to equal rights, justice, opportunities • Fiscal responsibility and budgetary restraint at all levels • Observing Constitutional limitations • Peace is best preserved through a strong national defense • Faith in God, as recognized by our Founding Fathers, is essential to the moral fiber of the Nation "We the People" are tired of politicians who do not see the dangers right in front of us; from out of control schools to election integrity. We want people that can clearly articulate what these issues are and boldly lead the people to action. If we elect leaders that hold fast to the Virginia Republican Creed, there is no way we can end up with runaway spending, crime in the streets, crushing regulations and an economy that favors the Big and Powerful over small business and our great citizens. "We the People" overwhelmingly love God, Country and our fellow citizens. But love does not mean that anything goes. We the People demand that politicians are accountable to us. They work for us. Senator Amanda Chase will listen to the vast majority who want truth and sanity back in Government. Unfortunately, that will take a fight, and we have someone who will fight the good fight to ensure that the City on a Hill remains bright for us and a light for generations to come." | Former Congressman Virgil Goode | | | " "I'm honored to support Senator Amanda Chase for re-election to the Virginia Senate. " | Delegate John McGuire | | "I'm proud to endorse Senator Amanda Chase for re-election to the Virginia Senate in district 12. Everyone knows Amanda is a fighter who stands strong and doesn't waiver on our conservative issues. " | "She has a lifelong 7 year voting record you can trust. 100 percent rating with the Family Foundation, 100 percent rating with the Virginia Citizens Defense League and an 'A' rating with the NRA. She's also been one of the top rated conservative Senators, if not the most conservative in most years, at CPAC by the American Conservative Union. As Chairwoman of the National Election Integrity Caucus she has led the efforts in Virginia to put the guardrails back on our elections and even introduced a bill calling for a full forensic audit of Virginia. It was an honor to work with her and successfully pass legislation that Governor Youngkin asked us to both carry to exempt retired military benefits for our veterans up to $40,000. This was a bill she originally introduced in the Senate in 2021 and I was proud to work with her and see this bill finally pass under a Republican Governor. " | Former Delegate Brenda Pogge | | "I endorse Senator Amanda Chase for re-election to the Virginia Senate in Distict 12. As a former delegate, I have served together with Amanda in the General Assembly and also on a commission. She is a strong reliable conservative voice and vote, and a woman of strong faith in God and the founding of our country on Judeo-Christian values. She not only votes to protect the unborn, she introduces legislation to defend life and clearly articulates why it's never the government's role to fund abortions with taxpayer dollars." | "Amanda is not a typical politician, she's a mom of 4 who deeply cares for the people in her community. Education and school choice is a top priority. She homeschooled her children when they were young and truly understands the importance of parents having input in their children's education and fully supports parental rights and choice in education over government mandates. She not only supports school choice, she lived it. As a senator, Amanda Chase has an impeccable 7 year conservative voting record. Whether you're talking about pro-life, gun rights, defending our Constitution and individual rights, fighting for real election integrity, voting for limited government, voting against all tax increases and fully funding our law enforcement, Senator Chase checks all the boxes. I stand with Senator Amanda Chase and I encourage you to do so too." | Bishop E.W. Jackson | | "I have known Senator Amanda Chase personally and professionally for over 15 years. It is my pleasure and honor to endorse her for re-election to the Virginia Senate in District 12. Senator Chase is a courageous Christian voice for faith, family, and freedom. | "Amanda is a bold champion for our conservative values and has an impeccable 7-year voting record to prove it. She is pro-God, pro-life, pro-2A, pro-family, pro-election integrity, pro-business, and pro-law and order. She is a pro-life leader for legislation in the Virginia Senate such as the pain capable and heart beat bills, and she has a 100 percent lifetime rating with the Family Foundation. Amanda exposed and spoke against the so-called Equal Rights Amendment that would enshrine abortion as well as gender identity politics into the Virginia Constitution. Senator Chase is not just a Republican placeholder; she's a conservative firebrand who is not afraid to speak truth to power. I stand with Amanda because she's a true conservative who stands with us." | Pennsylvania Senator Doug Mastriano | | | "I'm honored to endorse Amanda Chase for re-election to the Virginia Senate. As the founder of the National Election Integrity she's a champion for election integrity both nationally and in Virginia. She's a pleasure to work with and knows how to get the job done." Pennsylvania State Senator and Republican nominee Doug Mastriano | Arizona Senator Sonny Borelli | | "Senator Amanda Chase is the only Virginia legislator who actually came out to Arizona to watch our full forensic audit, and again when our full report was released to the Senate. She has my full support as she seeks re-election to the Virginia State Senate." | "It makes no sense that anyone claiming to be a Republican and who truly cares about election integrity would challenge her given everything she's done for election integrity, not just in Virginia but nationally. She's been actively engaged in getting answers to what happened in 2020, implementing those solutions in legislation for Virginia, to include a full forensic audit, and founding the National Election Integrity Caucus to support other legislators across the country. While we welcome anyone who wants to expose the lies and deception that took place in 2020, Amanda has been the leading work horse for election integrity in Virginia , not a show horse like her opponents who have recently taken an interest in putting the guardrails back on Virginia elections. If you truly support real election integrity I highly recommend you support Senator Amanda Chase for re-election to the Virginia Senate." | Colonial Heights City Councilman John Wood | | | "I am honored to support Senator Amanda Chase for re-election to the Virginia Senate." | Colonial Heights City Councilman Dr. Laura Poe | | "As a small business owner, professor, and a member of the Colonial Heights City Council, I'm proud to stand with and endorse Senator Chase in her run for re-election to the Virginia Senate in Senate District 12." | Senator Chase has represented my district since her election in 2016 and continues to push legislation to protect our individual freedoms and rights, such as overreaching COVID mandates, voting against tax increases, and preservation of the election process. She stands firm in her faith and positions amidst controversy and challenges. Her voting record is consistent and matches the promises she makes to her constituents. I support her continued service for District 12 as our Senator and provide her with my full endorsement." | Amelia County Sheriff Ricky Walker | | "I endorse Senator Amanda Chase as she seeks re-election to the Virginia Senate in district 12. Senator Chase has represented Amelia County for the past 7 years and I've had the pleasure to work with her on a number of issues." | "She's present in the community, an active listener who cares about public safety and keeping our community safe. She's an effective legislator who fully supports public safety, our sheriffs and law enforcement. Her 7 year voting record shows she supports the tools that help our sheriffs and law enforcement effectively do what they were trained to do to keep our communities safe. She's co-patroned pay raises for our sheriffs, voted to support qualified immunity, protected our law enforcement from personal financial devastation and spoken on the Senate floor about the importance of fully funding our law enforcement and against defunding the police. I stand with Senator Amanda Chase because she has a 7 year voting record of supporting our law enforcement." | Former Chesterfield Board of Supervisors Don Sowder | | | "I endorse Amanda Chase for a continuation of her successful results in putting the needs of her constituents ahead of going with the flow. We need Virginia Senators who put partisan politics aside and focus on people rather than politics. Amanda is just that person. She is courageously ahead of the curve in introducing and shepherding into law issues that meet peoples needs rather than seeking out easy and non-controversial issues. We need Amanda for another term in the Virginia Senate." | Former Chesterfield Commonwealth Attorney Billy Davenport | | "I have had the pleasure of knowing Senator Amanda Chase for the past fifteen years. She is a Christian lady who lives her life by "Founding Father Values", the values that made our Country great." | "She stands firm on our Constitutional Rights and amid much adversity she never wavers on the First and Second Amendments which give us the free exercise of religion, freedom of speech, the press, and the right to peacefully assemble to and petition the government for a redress of grievances, and the right to keep and bear arms. Without the first two amendments all the other amendments would have much less effect. She will not be silenced as she forcefully and effectively represents the people of her district. She is a true Statesman who will not violate her oath of office and the trust of her constituents." | Former Chesterfield Commissioner of Revenue Joe Horbal | | "I endorse Virginia State Senator Amanda Chase as she seeks re-election to the Senate of Virginia in Senate District 12. I've known Amanda both professionally and personally for over 15 years and know her to be a person of good character and integrity. " | "Even before she was in the state senate she consistently stood up for what was right and wasn't afraid to challenge the status quo. That said, she knows how to get things done and is a reliable and consistent vote for the people. She stands strongly for the Constitution and doing right by the people she represents. Amanda has been an effective Virginia State Senator who has consistently represented us well and without influence by special interest groups. She has accomplished much since she was first elected in 2015. She works tirelessly for the people to represent their needs and we need to re-elect her to the Virginia Senate to be our voice." | Dick Heller, D.C. vs. Heller, Supreme Court Gun Freedom Case | | | "I know of no harder working person in Virginia so capable and willing to fight for freedom and our foundational God-given natural rights through self-preservation through our United States Constitutional Second Amendment than Senator Amanda Chase." | You are invited to Join Team Chase | | Now that session is over, I can fully focus on running for re-election. If you're interested in scheduling a meet and greet please email Debbie at mailto:debbie@chaseforva.com. Also, if you'd like to help out, we're looking for people to collect signatures so that I can get my name on the ballot. I need 250 signatures. If you're interested, please reach out to Mike at mailto:mike@chaseforva.com. We hope to see you soon! For God & Country, | | SAVE THE DATE | FEBRUARY 25 | | | | == | MARCH 6 | Legislative Update for the 2023 Session | Please join us for an overview of the 2023 General Assembly Session. What happened during the legislative session? How does the legislation passed effect you? Things you need to know. Any legislator who would like to speak should reach out to Debbie at debbie@chaseforva.com to RSVP. | | MARCH 9 | Senate District 12 Candidate Debate | | MARCH 20 AT NOON | | In order to get my name on the ballot we must get at least 250 registered voters who live in == to sign my petition. This is something I have to do every time I've run for re-election. Thanks to all who have already signed and asked to collect signatures for us. If you'd like to sign my petition, you can stop by my senate office, see me at church on Sunday in the foyer in between services or attend one of our upcoming events like our Tea Party meetings, held on the first of each month. We can also email you the form. You can ask family members to sign the petition and mail the form back to us, but please remember to get the form notarized at your bank before you mail it back to us. We must turn in our signatures before Monday, March 20 at noon sharp to the GOP District Chair. You will want to turn in your notarized forms well before this date, as this is the date we must turn all forms into the chair. Please reach out to Mike at Mike@chaseforva.com if you have questions or call our campaign cell at 804 380 0009. | MARCH 31 | | APRIL 12 RECONVENE | | JUNE 20 | | | | | | | | == | | | == | | | == | | | == | | | == | | | == | | | == | | | == | | | == | | | == | | | == | | | == | | | == | | | == | | | == | Authorized and Paid for by Friends of Amanda Chase | | Friends of Amanda Chase | P.O. Box 5811, Midlothian, VA 23112 | | Sent by mailto:amanda@chaseforva.com powered by

China owns over 380,000 acres of American farmland, some only 12 miles away from an American military base, and we know why: To gain control over our agricultural industry and gather intelligence on our military capabilities. This HAS to stop. Do you want China controlling our food supply and gaining access to our military bases? Brad always says _"Food security is national security"._ [**Sign Brad's petition and DEMAND the Biden administration take action TODAY!**](https://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=0a899ad0193f48d399298a3556652e7c&_m=aa0cc515004f4cfaa59d96b717534cd7&_e=G1_sODMEqGiUH7VvTZNKL8GfgcVY15JJ1Jd-eoiqVcK53gXlBYhhsi1W1vroQYWeaY1-eJ6GBEnX11ndyHDi_PJpVVnRm37sYbOzZEBNetkCqiID6YtHcxx2-F0tVoeHnnHcwo-xJJhoYeo9fGxPevIR2xf96lTuNG_nK0hptVyEdPhr-ldGGZnaceTXOp0xyhZIPJQIPMII9CUrQsbNmK0zcF8gpsTKoAiJ4rk2Bh0EhkfsCkuRkwQK6sjqc46QwNz3s0x8d_1yqP9cXtIzTg%3D%3D)

clean_body: Demand Biden stop this!| | | This is concerning, friend. China owns over 380,000 acres of American farmland, some only 12 miles away from an American military base, and we know why: To gain control over our agricultural industry and gather intelligence on our military capabilities. This HAS to stop. Do you want China controlling our food supply and gaining access to our military bases? Brad always says "Food security is national security". It is unacceptable that the Biden Administration is continuing to stand by and allow China to have unabashed access to our land. Thank you, Aaron E. | | | | | | | | ---|---|---

name: Team Durbin
subject: Peter, did you hear?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Dick Durbin
body:
hold Big Tech accountable
#
Peter, did you hear that Senator Durbin reintroduced the _Clean Slate for Kids Online Act_?
The Senate Judiciary Committee recently held a hearing to examine just how harmful the online world can be for people, especially for the youngest members of our communities — our kids.
With internet companies collecting so much personal information these days, it's getting harder to keep our privacy intact.**But this new act would let every American demand that internet companies delete any info they collected from them when they were under the age of 13.**
**[If you agree that this is an important step to protect our families and hold Big Tech accountable for its dangerous practices, contribute $5 right now to show your support.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59246531/393682378/-984481996?refcode=dd_em_230225_1_t1&amount=5&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&addr1=&city=Englewood&state=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODQ4OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhZjk2MmE3Yy00MGI1LWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=CwG3M9Wharh6ktqsh_C0V9ZusOYbn5Z8qaRG66J169k=)**
Our kids should be able to start fresh when they're old enough to know the implications of their online activities. That's why the _Clean Slate for Kids Online Act_ is so important — it gives them that opportunity.
**[Will you pitch in $5 or whatever you can today to tell Congress to pass the _Clean Slate for Kids Online Act_? ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59246532/393682379/-984481996?refcode=dd_em_230225_1_t1&amount=5&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&addr1=&city=Englewood&state=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODQ4OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhZjk2MmE3Yy00MGI1LWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=CwG3M9Wharh6ktqsh_C0V9ZusOYbn5Z8qaRG66J169k=)**

Team Durbin

subject: Our goals for 2023

body:
|
---
Peter, We’re headed towards the end of the second month of the year, and 2023 is already flying by. After a better-than-expected2022 at the national level for Democrats, we’re looking to bring those same victories home to Indiana this year. Excellent candidates are running in cities and towns across Indiana, and we have an opportunity to create real change at the state and local level by doing well this year. [**Help us hit our end of month fundraising goal for February by chipping in to the Indiana Democratic Party today. Your contribution will go towards making sure we have good people in office, representing our best interests, and fighting to create a better state for everyone.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59246754/393682846/1643342139?refcode=230225-FR&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzYzE4NzczYi00MmI1LWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=kbip8gLbtnLDzYN1ypd2kJzwSDo3IjGGRcEYV7r4svM=) After nearly two decades of Republican state administrations, Indiana needs a change. name: Aadland For Colorado
subject: Reminder: Service Matters
body:
From Erik Aadland
| | | |
---
---
|
# My Service Matters
---
|
---
| | |
---|---
|
------My service matters in this election because my character has been tested on the battlefield. There are very few who have gone to the lengths I have to protect and defend this country. What we have done in our past is a testament to who we are; credentials and our personal history matters because it speaks to what we are capable of, how we conducted ourselves, and what we are likely to do in the future. No other candidate in this race even comes close to my level of service to country, or personal accomplishments, yet they would try to reduce it for personal and political gain
| |
---|---
|
------As a young man, my deepest desire was to serve my country, so after much effort to get accepted, I attended the United States Military Academy at West Point where I first took my oath to support and defend the U.S. Constitution. 9-11 happened when I was a senior, so I knew then that war was in my future. I was a tank platoon leader in Iraq, where I earned a Bronze Star for Valor (with V-device) over the course of hundreds of combat patrols. It was my brigade that captured Saddam Hussein. In Afghanistan, I commanded a Stryker Reconnaissance Troop, in charge of securing the border crossing point between Afghanistan and Pakistan, in the Kandahar District city of Spin Boldak; the second most strategically important location in the country. I earned another Bronze Star there. I was always assigned the toughest missions because my commanders knew I would get it done right. I have risked my life for this country on numerous occasions, demonstrating the fullest dedication and willingness to make the ultimate sacrifice for our freedom.
------With politicians falsely claiming valor where there is none, mistaking loudly spoken words for courage, it is time to return to the dignity, respect, and virtue of our foundational Republican values. My past history speaks to how I will conduct myself in the future. As Chairman, I will strive for excellence and act with the highest integrity, courage, honor, and respect in my quest to restore our party to greatness.
---
|
---
|
---
| |
---
---
| | [Send Erik your feedback](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001BaN1ECiSVgRKog8ufw6gdrGwMKO-jX5hkcXfdy40o15DU9TFqnMc47_kwL6HcdNYHXu7GH_ZxhxLeVeJDv91iwNl9ly9PcoibFb4v5SteUQ4PeiFhn6zxRTUcJo-DUFrnzErWjlATXymdZ83ir8c2QlXdkZk6Mr4QKFI9zjL-0FdB9WQTTzQJmjO4TNgD27-dxh37i4EjpA=&c=g61vlf_2lSEp-SaR6-QRcbhVRr4Y4wyn3t-RzRlgpE1hTfDj8JyfDQ==&ch=jHKtdLvfiVGc-u5RX2ZymnwLKPmgNAxwePF8dDiV75PynSLJ_e3wqw==)
---
|
---
---
|
## About Erik
---
| |
---
| Jefferson County resident Erik Aadland is a former Congressional Candidate (CO-7), West Point graduate and a twice-decorated Bronze Star veteran--one for valor--from his time in Iraq and Afghanistan. Concluding his army career in 2011 as a Captain, he pursued a career in the oil and natural gas industry with his work culminating as a project manager directing the onshore construction of the Leviathan Project in Dor, Israel. This project resulted in Israel being energy-independent and a net exporter of natural gas. Erik holds a master's degree in the field of psychology. He is a devoted husband and proud father of three children.

clean_body: From Erik Aadland | | | | | My Service Matters | | | | ---|--- | ------My service matters in this election because my character has been tested on the battlefield. There are very few who have gone to the lengths I have to protect and defend this country. What we have done in our past is a testament to who we are; credentials and our personal history matters because it speaks to what we are capable of, how we conducted ourselves, and what we are likely to do in the future. No other candidate in this race even comes close to my level of service to country, or personal accomplishments, yet they would try to reduce it for personal and political gain | | ---|--- | ------As a young man, my deepest desire was to serve my country, so after much effort to get accepted, I attended the United States Military Academy at West Point where I first took my oath to support and defend the U.S. Constitution. 9-11 happened when I was a senior, so I knew then that war was in my future. I was a tank platoon leader in Iraq, where I earned a Bronze Star for Valor (with V-device) over the course of hundreds of combat patrols. It was my brigade that captured Saddam Hussein. In Afghanistan, I commanded a Stryker Reconnaissance Troop, in charge of securing the border crossing point between Afghanistan and Pakistan, in the Kandahar District city of Spin Boldak; the second most strategically important location in the country. I earned another Bronze Star there. I was always assigned the toughest missions because my commanders knew I would get it done right. I have risked my life for this country on numerous occasions, demonstrating the fullest dedication and willingness to make the ultimate sacrifice for our freedom. ------With politicians falsely claiming valor where there is none, mistaking loudly spoken words for courage, it is time to return to the dignity, respect, and virtue of our foundational Republican values. My past history speaks to how I will conduct myself in the future. As Chairman, I will strive for excellence and act with the highest integrity, courage, honor, and respect in my quest to restore our party to greatness. | | | | | | == | | About Erik | | | Jefferson County resident Erik Aadland is a former Congressional Candidate (CO-7), West Point graduate and a twice-decorated Bronze Star veteran--one for valor--from his time in Iraq and Afghanistan. Concluding his army career in 2011 as a Captain, he pursued a career in the oil and natural gas industry with his work culminating as a project manager directing the onshore construction of the Leviathan Project in Dor, Israel. This project resulted in Israel being energy-independent and a net exporter of natural gas. Erik holds a master's degree in the field of psychology. He is a devoted husband and proud father of three children. | | == == == | PO Box 630552 | Littleton, CO 80163 tel:720-464-4488 == | | | | | | | Aadland for Colorado | PO Box 630552, Littleton, CO 80163 | (720) 464-4488 | | Sent by mailto:staff@aadlandforcolorado.com in collaboration with

name: Vivek Ramaswamy
subject: What does it mean to be an American in 2023?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Vivek 2024
body:
| [](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/62364828:JMHLmcGEN:m:1:3331421248:4768781B79D2FA3F69DAFF6D7FC6F635:r)
Peter,

I am lucky. I grew up in Ohio in the '90s back when America still valued merit.

I might have been a skinny kid with a funny last name, but my parents taught me an important lesson: _If you are going to stand out, you might as well be outstanding._

If I'd been born 20 years later, my story would have been impossible. Today our nation is hungry for a cause; we want purpose and meaning.

The woke Left preys on that vacuum with a poisonous obsession with race, sexuality, and climate change. If you disagree with them, they call you a bigot. This is psychological slavery.

The best measure of the health of American democracy is the percentage of citizens who feel free to say what they actually think - _**IN PUBLIC.**_

Right now, we're failing that test.

**That's why I am running for President of the United States.**

[](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/62364828:JMHLmcGEN:m:1:3331421248:4768781B79D2FA3F69DAFF6D7FC6F635:r)
Today, we're starting a cultural movement in our country, and I want you to be a part of it. I want you to join our movement to create a new American dream for the next generation.

This time it isn't just about money. It is about the unapologetic pursuit of excellence itself. It means that we:

Believe in merit.
Believe in accountability.
Believe in free speech.
Believe in American exceptionalism.
If you're on board with these ideals, then we're already on the same team. We're ready to fight for the future of America. If we rediscover our shared national identity, then we can take on the greatest external threat we face: Communist China.

**[Will you join me? Please take just a few minutes to watch my launch video- and join Team Vivek by chipping in $25 or even $50 today as I launch my presidential campaign.](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/62364828:JMHLmcGEN:m:1:3331421248:4768781B79D2FA3F69DAFF6D7FC6F635:r)**

It's time to stand up to the ruling aristocracy in America - in Washington D.C., on Wall Street, and in Silicon Valley - that does whatever allows them to accumulate more money and power for themselves. They try to divide & conquer, but that won't work if we stay united.

**E pluribus unum - _from many, one._**

These are the ideals that won the American Revolution; that reunited us after the Civil War; that won two World Wars and the Cold War. I believe deep in my bones these ideals still exist, and I am running for President to revive them.

Join me,

[](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/62364828:JMHLmcGEN:m:1:3331421248:4768781B79D2FA3F69DAFF6D7FC6F635:r)
Vivek Ramaswamy

| [** WATCH NOW ->**](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/62364828:JMHLmcGEN:m:1:3331421248:4768781B79D2FA3F69DAFF6D7FC6F635:r)
---

**GET TO KNOW VIVEK:** Vivek is an American entrepreneur, political and cultural thought leader, and New York Times bestselling author of **_Woke, Inc.: Inside Corporate America's Social Justice Scam_** , along with his second book, **_Nation of Victims: Identity Politics, the Death of Merit, and the Path Back to Excellence_**.

He has been a prominent and outspoken critic against ESG, discriminatory practices under the guise of critical race theory, and of what he calls "government-tech censorship," where the government colludes with big tech to do through the backdoor what it couldn't do through the front door under the Constitution.

Vivek is a first-generation American. Growing up in the Cincinnati area, he often recounts the sage advice he received from his father that "If you're going to stand out, then you might as well be outstanding." This advice set the course for his life: an accomplished pianist, a nationally ranked tennis player, and the valedictorian of his Jesuit high school. He went on to graduate summa cum laude in Biology from Harvard in 2007 and received his J.D. degree from Yale Law School in 2013, worked at a hedge fund, then started a pharmaceutical company, Roivant Sciences, where his team developed five drugs that went on to become FDA-approved.

In 2022, he founded Strive Asset Management, an Ohio-based firm, that takes direct aim at asset managers like BlackRock, State Street, Vanguard and others for their fiduciary breach of duty to maximize the wealth of their clients for Wokeism. Strive's mission is to restore the voices of everyday citizens in the American economy by leading companies to focus on excellence over politics.

[ ** STAND WITH VIVEK ➞ **](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/62364828:JMHLmcGEN:m:1:3331421248:4768781B79D2FA3F69DAFF6D7FC6F635:r)

---

| [](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/62364827:JMHLmcGEN:m:1:3331421248:4768781B79D2FA3F69DAFF6D7FC6F635:r) | [](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/62364826:JMHLmcGEN:m:1:3331421248:4768781B79D2FA3F69DAFF6D7FC6F635:r) | [](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/62364825:JMHLmcGEN:m:1:3331421248:4768781B79D2FA3F69DAFF6D7FC6F635:r)

Our organization runs on the generosity of people from all over Ohio and the country who are passionate about our shared values and our belief in a government that honors the Dignity of Work. Together, we’ve won tough races in the past on the strength of that movement. Because when an autoworker in Toledo [_**chips in $26**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59246875/393683164/380169402?refcode=sbe230225&amount=26&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTMvMS84NzY0NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlN2MwZGI2MS00M2I1LWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Gw2W38XntCKUvPx-L_qGzDzD3rjVEue78_BTXBl0Gtc=) and a nurse in Columbus [_**pitches in $26**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59246876/393683165/380169402?refcode=sbe230225&amount=26&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTMvMS84NzY0NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlN2MwZGI2MS00M2I1LWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Gw2W38XntCKUvPx-L_qGzDzD3rjVEue78_BTXBl0Gtc=) and a teacher in Steubenville [_**contributes $26**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59246877/393683166/380169402?refcode=sbe230225&amount=26&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTMvMS84NzY0NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlN2MwZGI2MS00M2I1LWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Gw2W38XntCKUvPx-L_qGzDzD3rjVEue78_BTXBl0Gtc=), it adds up to a powerful coalition that has what it takes to compete in a competitive, must-win battleground. That’s what we’re facing in the coming election. Our race is one of the most competitive Senate contests on the map in 2024. The national GOP is already targeting me, and my first opponent is a wealthy state senator who bankrolled his campaign during last year’s Ohio Senate race -- including millions of dollars spent on ads so he could dominate the airwaves. [_**That’s OK -- one contribution at a time, we can out-organize our opponents, the GOP, and their dark-money machine. But that’s why I need to ask: Will you chip in before tomorrow?**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59246878/393683167/380169402?refcode=sbe230225&amount=26&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTMvMS84NzY0NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlN2MwZGI2MS00M2I1LWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Gw2W38XntCKUvPx-L_qGzDzD3rjVEue78_BTXBl0Gtc=) With gratitude, Sherrod

name: DLCC Online Fundraising Team
subject: Devastating setback:
body:
we’ll make this quick because we don’t have much time to waste. | | |
---
| | We’ll make this quick because we don’t have much time to waste. Right now, we’re way behind on our February goal to elect state Democrats, and if we miss this goal, the consequences will be huge. **We still need $63,492 before our February end-of-month deadline to get back on track.** This couldn’t be a more critical time – there’s no off-year in state politics, and we have a dozen special elections on the books for the next few months. And just this week, we got some bad news: State Republicans are dumping money into attack ads into ultra-competitive Virginia and spilling six figures into special elections. **The pressure is on to close this fundraising gap to stay competitive.** So we’re asking you directly – [can we count on you to be one of the local Democrats we need to donate a triple-matched $7 or whatever you’re able to afford right now to help us bridge our fundraising gap?](https://an.dlcc.org/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953zPxM5q-4kK8uu6lZcSOahK4InhYdFXlzaxnqq0uP-l1F6LDtW_T8nq6ZooqDwO-83tbwudwV86xzfdYWJ9rMk6nZ9gEITcURHy6JpwXWwgeLiMclCxYAh0FUZ24sjL7s2T-fe5C5t3tHpTksFrN4WDZxW5R3zFya6E8nZQ4_GkWyC4C5VIt4mLK9Zrp1yI9tXbrDDVcOYy84bQZkwaytWbI23lmmyMzyI1pZaWy7fEASj5KDEENEaIOHQbRa8MCmhRDX2TFVmB6S143gqXd3UaP0a0nSscZwZq_B2jSS9K6o-9rZpjvFnbqeEpfUgyrCW4mH1Hjb1pqch2-xKXopon1jmhdt2PJVXcUEV-2Y1_kIOkiVwYGupdskMUDgDm0ta2lfckByaLwUtOYxR8_GpuLFpDR3awRAj2lZQX4aJaZYuFOosIIGIMxogpMsLmz8cx91__9QYX5b103PSdg4g0I5k_ow8NaAsY08ckS6awtSF9A_fpjf-rJI7loxpsUNZZtTdoVW2_CvDLqH3RVA4h/3u0/Rxb3OqliRaW27g8x8--5xw/h0/EJBxVBEr94uz5QQMczBg2Dc6dSDVf9CBDaVI49oGWg8) Your donations are critical in powering our work because when we hit our goals, we’re able to budget, plan, and strategize months in advance of special elections – making our campaigns more effective, connected, and reflective of voters’ needs in our most competitive districts. | If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process immediately.

Our work to elect state Democrats isn’t easy – we’re up against Republican gerrymandering, Trump loyalists, and right-wing billionaire-fueled campaigns. These obstacles aren’t easy to overcome – but they're not insurmountable if everyone on this grassroots team comes together to do their part.
That’s why we’re fighting tooth and nail to hit each and every fundraising goal right now. State legislative elections often fly under the radar, and sometimes come down to just a handful of votes, so a little investment goes a long way. clean_body: we’ll make this quick because we don’t have much time to waste. | | | | | We’ll make this quick because we don’t have much time to waste. Right now, we’re way behind on our February goal to elect state Democrats, and if we miss this goal, the consequences will be huge. We still need $63,492 before our February end-of-month deadline to get back on track. This couldn’t be a more critical time – there’s no off-year in state politics, and we have a dozen special elections on the books for the next few months. And just this week, we got some bad news: State Republicans are dumping money into attack ads into ultra-competitive Virginia and spilling six figures into special elections. The pressure is on to close this fundraising gap to stay competitive. So we’re asking you directly – Your donations are critical in powering our work because when we hit our goals, we’re able to budget, plan, and strategize months in advance of special elections – making our campaigns more effective, connected, and reflective of voters’ needs in our most competitive districts. | If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process immediately. | | | | | Our work to elect state Democrats isn’t easy – we’re up against Republican gerrymandering, Trump loyalists, and right-wing billionaire-fueled campaigns. These obstacles aren’t easy to overcome – but they're not insurmountable if everyone on this grassroots team comes together to do their part. That’s why we’re fighting tooth and nail to hit each and every fundraising goal right now. Peter, over the course of **44 hours, three mass shootings in California claimed the lives of 19 people. **

The first tragedy was in Monterey Park, where a celebration of the Lunar New Year in the heart of the city’s Asian American community ended in 11 lives lost. The next was in a rural seaside town in northern California, where seven immigrant laborers were killed. And the third tragedy in less than 48 hours was in Oakland, where one person was killed and seven others were wounded during the filming of a music video.

**Our nation’s bloody history of gun violence has proven time and time again that assault weapons are the weapon of choice for mass shooters – and yet we are _still_ fighting tooth and nail against the gun lobby and its favorite politicians to get these military-style weapons of war off the streets of America. **
**Peter, I refuse to back down from this fight, but I’m counting on fearless fighters like _you_**** __ to stand with me. That’s why I urgently need at least 10,000 supporters to recommit themselves to our fight against gun violence.**

So please, before this poll closes at midnight, will you respond to join the fight against gun violence and call on Congress to take action?
| | **Should Congress ban military-style assault weapons?**
---
| | | [YES](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59247568/393686585/2106823967?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&code=jtk991-su-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS84OTg0OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0ZTc3MTBmYi00NmI1LWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=C-knGjj-06XJ1P7c2Dar4Twe8vu4KUpzMUQQ8yyjvtM=&emci=d5f85827-06b2-ed11-994d-00224832e1ba&emdi=4e7710fb-46b5-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&ceid=25369504)

clean_body: | | | | | Should Congress ban military-style assault weapons? | | | | Peter, over the course of 44 hours, three mass shootings in California claimed the lives of 19 people. The first tragedy was in Monterey Park, where a celebration of the Lunar New Year in the heart of the city’s Asian American community ended in 11 lives lost. The next was in a rural seaside town in northern California, where seven immigrant laborers were killed. And the third tragedy in less than 48 hours was in Oakland, where one person was killed and seven others were wounded during the filming of a music video. Our nation’s bloody history of gun violence has proven time and time again that assault weapons are the weapon of choice for mass shooters – and yet we are still fighting tooth and nail against the gun lobby and its favorite politicians to get these military-style weapons of war off the streets of America. Peter, I refuse to back down from this fight, but I’m counting on fearless fighters like you to stand with me. That’s why I urgently need at least 10,000 supporters to recommit themselves to our fight against gun violence. So please, before this poll closes at midnight, will you respond to join the fight against gun violence and call on Congress to take action? | | Should Congress ban military-style assault weapons? | | | | Thank you, Letitia | This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. body:
We wanted to make sure you saw this from President Biden. If you agree with him, show your support for electing Democrats up and down the ballot by chipping in before Tuesday’s fundraising deadline. | |
